title: Aureline work-item provider-object identity, authority, sync-freshness, and mapping-provenance vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the provider_object_record frozen in docs/work_items/provider_object_and_traceability_contract.md. One record family pins a typed projection of a single provider-side object (issue, task, story, bug, incident, change request, RFC, or other) so every Aureline surface that reads or shows the object renders a consistent provider-authority status, a consistent sync-freshness chip, a consistent mapping-provenance posture, and a per-field truth row that tells the user which fields are provider-authoritative, which are locally proposed (draft overlay pending publish), which are cached read-only shadow, which are imported-handoff snapshots, and which are mapping-conflict unresolved. The record never carries raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw label values that disclose customer / tenant identity, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, or raw notification payloads; rows carry opaque ids, typed vocabulary, redaction-aware reviewable labels (<= 1024 graphemes), and freshness / authority / mapping / origin tokens only. Composes with (and does not replace) the work_item_detail_record schema (the per-row header that aggregates state, owner, freshness, write-authority, and engineering relations); the provider-mode contract (mutation_mode, surface_class, provider_actor_class, redaction_class, drift_state_class, next_safe_action_class, relation_class are re-exported from there); the publish-later contract (provider_object_relation_record, account_mapping_binding_record, provider_callback_envelope_record); the connected-provider registry (connected_provider_record_id_ref); and the offline-handoff packet (linked_offline_handoff_packet_record_id_ref for imported evidence). The eventual work-items crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new record_kind, provider_authority_status_class, sync_freshness_class, mapping_provenance_class, provider_field_class, field_origin_class, mapping_conflict_origin_class, denial_reason, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and bumps provider_object_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provider_object_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the provider-object vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, and raw notification payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary safe for support / audit / queue-review / admin-reconciliation surfaces; never carries the raw payload.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, queue-review, and export review surfaces. Never carries the raw payload.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the provider-object projection was admitted under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class re-exported from ADR-0010 / ADR-0007. Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, and raw notification payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

object_class.description: Object class on the provider. Re-exported from schemas/work_items/work_item_detail.schema.json so the provider-object projection and the work-item header read the same vocabulary. 'other' is repair-only.

provider_host.description: Opaque provider host handle (registry row id, not raw hostname). Empty only when authority is local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object.

tenant_or_org_scope.description: Opaque tenant / org scope handle. Empty only when authority is local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the projection references.

provider_authority_status_class.description: Frozen authority status for the provider-object projection. provider_authoritative_synced means the local cache is in sync and the provider is the source of truth; provider_authoritative_stale_local_continues means the freshness floor has crossed and the projection is still readable but no longer fresh; local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object means the projection was authored locally and never sent; cached_read_only_shadow_inspect_only means the projection is an inspect-only shadow (e.g. policy-blocked write scope); imported_evidence_only_no_live_provider_path means the projection was reconstructed from a support export / incident bundle / offline-handoff with no live provider path; mapping_conflict_unresolved means the local and provider sides disagree on at least one field and the user has not yet resolved the conflict; mapping_pending_user_resolution means the account mapping is awaiting user / admin selection and the projection has no active provider-object overlay.

sync_freshness_class.description: Frozen sync-freshness posture re-exported from schemas/work_items/work_item_detail.schema.json. degraded_beyond_grace_local_continues, unverifiable_provider_unreachable, and imported_snapshot_no_refresh_path are repair-aware lanes that MUST NOT be relabelled as live-authoritative-fresh. local_draft_never_published is admissible only on local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object authority.

mapping_provenance_class.description: Frozen mapping-provenance class. Names where the local <-> provider mapping the projection reads against came from. provider_callback_envelope_validated and account_mapping_binding_resolved are the two provider-validated lanes; account_mapping_binding_pending is the pending-resolution lane; user_authored_local_draft_no_provider_object pins local-draft authority; the four imported_from_* lanes pin imported-evidence authority; cached_read_only_shadow_no_refresh pins cached-shadow authority; mapping_conflict_unresolved_local_vs_provider pins mapping-conflict authority.

provider_field_class.description: Frozen provider-field class the projection's per-field row represents. Surfaces preserve the provider's exact upstream value through field_value_label rather than collapsing into a packet-local enum, but every row names exactly one of these axes so consumers can render them mechanically. 'other_provider_owned_field' is repair-only.

field_origin_class.description: Frozen field-origin class. provider_authoritative_field is the verbatim provider-side value at last fresh import; locally_proposed_overlay_pending_publish is a value the user authored as a local draft over the provider value (or in absence of one) and that has not yet been published; cached_read_only_shadow_field is a snapshotted value the local row is forbidden to mutate; imported_handoff_field_no_refresh is a value snapshotted from an imported handoff with no refresh path; mapping_conflict_unresolved_field is a row where the provider-side value and the locally-proposed overlay disagree and the user has not yet resolved; derived_from_linked_review_field and derived_from_linked_change_field are values derived from a linked review-pack evaluation result or change-object landing state, not a direct provider import.

field_value_label.description: Reviewable label of the active value the surface should render. For mapping_conflict_unresolved_field rows, surfaces SHOULD render both provider_authoritative_value_label and local_overlay_value_label rather than picking one as the active value.

provider_authoritative_value_label.description: Reviewable label of the provider-side authoritative value. Required on mapping_conflict_unresolved_field rows. Empty otherwise.

local_overlay_value_label.description: Reviewable label of the locally-proposed overlay value. Required on locally_proposed_overlay_pending_publish and mapping_conflict_unresolved_field rows. Empty otherwise.

source_schema_ref.description: Opaque ref to the upstream schema / contract that minted the value (e.g. schemas/vcs/review_workspace.schema.json for derived_from_linked_review_field). Empty when the value has no upstream schema beyond the provider itself.

source_field.description: Optional opaque field name within the upstream schema.

source_ref.description: Opaque ref to the upstream record the value was sourced from (e.g. review_workspace_record_id_ref, change_object_record_id_ref).

allOf.description: locally_proposed_overlay_pending_publish field rows MUST cite a non-empty local_overlay_value_label so the user can see the proposed value that has not yet been published.
mapping_conflict_unresolved_field rows MUST cite both a provider_authoritative_value_label and a local_overlay_value_label so the user can see exactly which side disagrees with which value.

provider_field_row.description: One per-field row preserving the provider's (or local-overlay's / cached-shadow's / imported-handoff's / linked-review's) exact field vocabulary plus a typed origin and a reviewable sentence. Multiple rows are allowed; the same field_class MAY appear at most once per record.

mapping_conflict_origin_class.description: Frozen mapping-conflict origin class. Pins why the local and provider sides disagree on at least one field. account_remap_conflict means the connected-account changed between captures; provider_object_split_conflict / provider_object_merge_conflict mean the provider side restructured the object; the three freshness_drift_conflict_* lanes mean the upstream object drifted past the local snapshot (one side, the other side, or both); actor_scope_changed_conflict means the bound actor scope narrowed; policy_epoch_rolled_conflict means a policy epoch rolled between projections; imported_handoff_replay_conflict means an imported handoff replayed against a live provider object that has since changed.

next_safe_action_class.description: Next-safe-action hint re-exported (and extended for mapping conflicts) from the publish-later contract's next_safe_action_class. Surfaces MUST render this and MUST NOT invent surface-local actions outside this set. resolve_mapping_conflict is the only admissible next-safe-action when provider_authority_status_class is mapping_conflict_unresolved; no_action_imported_evidence_only is the only admissible next-safe-action on imported_evidence_only_no_live_provider_path authority.

conflict_field_classes.description: Field classes that disagree between provider and local sides. Every entry MUST also appear as a mapping_conflict_unresolved_field row in provider_field_rows.

next_safe_action_class.description: Next-safe-action the surface should offer. MUST be one of resolve_mapping_conflict, refresh_before_edit, escalate_for_admin_review, reselect_account, or open_in_provider_via_browser_handoff.

linked_provider_callback_envelope_record.description: Opaque ref to the provider_callback_envelope_record that observed the conflicting upstream value. Empty when the conflict was detected from a stale local cache rather than a live callback.

linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the account_mapping_binding_record bound to the conflict (e.g. for account_remap_conflict). Empty when the conflict is unrelated to account mapping.

linked_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to a browser_handoff_packet_record the user can use to view the provider-side state. Empty when no handoff is offered.

mapping_conflict_block.description: Typed conflict block surfaced when provider_authority_status_class is mapping_conflict_unresolved. Every conflicting field class MUST also appear as a mapping_conflict_unresolved_field row in provider_field_rows.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the provider-object projection was minted under. Reused identically by the work_item_detail and traceability_link schemas.

policy_context.description: Policy context the provider-object projection was admitted under. Revalidated on every callback envelope refresh and on every mapping-conflict resolution.

denial_reason.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary. A surface that admits a projection that violates one of the gates emits a provider_object_audit_event_record citing the matching reason. Silent downgrade is forbidden.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event vocabulary for the provider-object projection. The provider-side handoff stream remains the ADR-0010 provider_handoff stream; this stream is the local-only provider-object lifecycle stream.

connected_provider_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the connected_provider_record. Empty string only when provider_authority_status_class is local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object.

freshness_observed_at.description: Monotonic timestamp of the most recent provider import / local-draft commit / imported-handoff snapshot the freshness chip resolves against.

freshness_floor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the freshness floor the projection resolves against. Empty when the authority is local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object.

provider_field_rows.description: Per-field truth rows. At minimum one row is required so the projection always discloses at least one field origin. The same field_class MAY appear at most once.

mapping_conflict_block.description: Conflict block. Required (and only admissible) when provider_authority_status_class is mapping_conflict_unresolved.

linked_provider_object_relation_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound provider_object_relation_record from schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json. Required for provider-bound authority statuses (provider_authoritative_synced, provider_authoritative_stale_local_continues, cached_read_only_shadow_inspect_only) so a downstream surface always reads the relation chip mechanically.

linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the bound account_mapping_binding_record from schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json. Required when provider_authority_status_class is mapping_pending_user_resolution. Empty when actor resolution is resolved.

linked_provider_callback_envelope_record.description: Opaque refs to provider_callback_envelope_records that refreshed the projection. Empty when no provider callback has been observed.

linked_offline_handoff_packet_record_id_.description: Opaque ref to the bound offline_handoff_packet_record from schemas/work_items/offline_handoff_packet.schema.json. Required when mapping_provenance_class is imported_from_offline_handoff_packet_no_live_provider_path.

linked_object_handoff_packet_record_id_r.description: Opaque ref to the bound object_handoff_packet_record from schemas/support/object_handoff_packet.schema.json. Required when mapping_provenance_class is imported_from_object_handoff_packet_no_live_provider_path.

linked_support_bundle_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound support_bundle the projection was reconstructed from. Required when mapping_provenance_class is imported_from_support_export_no_live_provider_path.

linked_incident_workspace_packet_record_.description: Opaque ref to the bound incident_workspace_packet the projection was reconstructed from. Required when mapping_provenance_class is imported_from_incident_workspace_no_live_provider_path.

connected_work_item_detail_record_id_ref.description: Opaque refs to the work_item_detail_records that read this projection. A single provider_object_record MAY back multiple detail rows (e.g. across workspaces). Empty when no detail row has been admitted yet.

supersedes_provider_object_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to a prior provider_object_record this one supersedes (e.g. after an account remap or a provider-side merge). Empty when this is a fresh row.

allOf.description: provider_authoritative_synced, provider_authoritative_stale_local_continues, and cached_read_only_shadow_inspect_only authority MUST cite a non-empty linked_provider_object_relation_record_id_ref so a downstream surface always reads the relation class mechanically.
mapping_pending_user_resolution authority MUST cite a non-empty linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id_ref so the user / admin reconciliation console can pick up the pending mapping.
mapping_conflict_unresolved authority MUST cite a non-null mapping_conflict_block.
imported_evidence_only_no_live_provider_path authority MUST resolve sync_freshness_class to imported_snapshot_no_refresh_path and mapping_provenance_class to one of the imported_from_* lanes.

local_shadow_draft_no_provider_object authority MUST resolve sync_freshness_class to local_draft_never_published or warm_within_grace and mapping_provenance_class to user_authored_local_draft_no_provider_object.
mapping_conflict_unresolved authority MUST resolve mapping_provenance_class to mapping_conflict_unresolved_local_vs_provider.
mapping_pending_user_resolution authority MUST resolve mapping_provenance_class to account_mapping_binding_pending.
cached_read_only_shadow_inspect_only authority MUST forbid locally_proposed_overlay_pending_publish field rows; cached read-only shadow projections MUST NOT carry a local overlay.
imported_evidence_only_no_live_provider_path authority MUST forbid locally_proposed_overlay_pending_publish field rows; imported-evidence projections MUST NOT carry a live local overlay.
imported_from_support_export_no_live_provider_path provenance MUST cite a non-empty linked_support_bundle_record_id_ref.
mapping_conflict_unresolved authority MUST resolve next_safe_action_class to resolve_mapping_conflict.
imported_evidence_only_no_live_provider_path authority MUST resolve next_safe_action_class to no_action_imported_evidence_only.

provider_object_record.description: Frozen provider-object projection record. Composes with the work-item detail header through connected_work_item_detail_record_id_refs (back-refs); with the provider-mode contract through linked_provider_object_relation_record_id_ref / linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id_ref / linked_provider_callback_envelope_record_id_refs; and with the imported-evidence path through linked_offline_handoff_packet_record_id_ref / linked_object_handoff_packet_record_id_ref / linked_support_bundle_record_id_ref / linked_incident_workspace_packet_record_id_ref. The eventual work-items crate's Rust types are the source of truth; this is the cross-tool boundary.

allOf.description: provider_object_audit_denial_emitted MUST cite a denial reason.

provider_object_audit_event_record.description: Audit-event row for the provider-object projection. Denial events MUST cite a typed reason from the closed denial vocabulary; lifecycle events (admitted, authority status changed, freshness changed, field origin changed, mapping conflict detected / resolved, callback observed, account mapping changed, superseded) carry no denial reason.
